Dragonball Z Series Online
 

Posted By: jamjar
Date: Wednesday 1st September, 2004 at 21:12 GMT
  
Hey, sorry for the long wait, I have finally finished uploading the whole of the dragonballz series to the site. I'll post another update later on this week, I have virtually no time now as i'm trying to get ready for the start of school. Also, thanks for the donations last month, unfortunately it was nowhere near enough, but it still made a difference. Hopefully this month we will reach the goal. Thanks :)
Donations Please
 

Posted By: jamjar
Date: Sunday 29th August, 2004 at 12:45 GMT
  
Hey, as you can probably see it is coming toward the end of the month and we are nowhere near our monthly donations goal. If you download stuff from the website, atleast try and donate something as it costs alot of money to keep the media server up and running. We accept donations through paypal which means you can send us money from your credit card, bank account or paypal account. Here are DETAILS ON HOW TO DONATE. If you are only able to send cash, please e-mail me and I will send you the address details.

Also be sure to check out our MONTHLY PRIZE DRAW where each month you can win an anime dvd. This month it is Neon Genesis Evangelion - End of Evangelion.
Media Back Up + Some Anime Reviews + Monthly Competition
 

Posted By: jamjar
Date: Friday 27th August, 2004 at 12:29 GMT
  
Hey, the wait is over; the movies are up and so are the dragonball gt episodes, we will have episodes from other animes up some time over the next few days. Some have you may have noticed that some pages were not loading last night and early this morning; it's just a slight technical propblem with our main server and will not effect the media - should be fixed within a few hours. Also thanks to Dante we have five new anime reviews for you to read. If you want to request a review, e-mail either dante or spike.

Be sure to check out our NEW MONTHLY PRIZE DRAW where each month you can win an anime dvd. This month it is Neon Genesis Evangelion - End of Evangelion.
Few Small Bits + Update on Media
 

Posted By: jamjar
Date: Wednesday 25th August, 2004 at 15:09 GMT
  
Hey, I've added a few new things to the multimedia section; some flash games (Interactive) and some anime music downloads. If you're ofteb bored out of your mind and enjoy surfing the web; I'm sure you'll like the interactive section. Hmm...What else....I've added a link to the forums on the side menu (replacing the fan section)...we haven't had any fanart sent in for a while - i'll probably put the fan section under miscelaneous. As for the media; everything is going to plan. By the end of today I will have everything up except for the dragonballz and naurto episodes. As you know we will be doing a major content release this friday (Including the Media), so be sure to check back then. If you havn't aleady, please check out the FORUMS
Good News: Forum + Media
 

Posted By: jamjar
Date: Tuesday 24th August, 2004 at 11:58 GMT
  
Hey, finally I have some good news for you guys. We have managed to find a new media host; It will cost $150/month extra, but it should be worth it, the first few bits of media should be up by tonight....Even more good news; the forums are now up and running: CLICK HERE - you can start posting if you want, I'll add a link to the navigation bare on friday (their official release). Bare in mind some tweaking still needs to be done. Please keep those donations coming :)
Forum Moderators Needed + New Music Video
 

Posted By: jamjar
Date: Sunday 22nd August, 2004 at 18:17 GMT
  
Hey, I've put up a new music video; which was sent to me by a fan (The One) last week. Now onto the main topic of this post...We are almost ready to launch the dbgt forums and are looking for some people to help moderate them. If you are interested please e-mail me giving details of your age, interests, favourite anime's and experience with forums in the past.

A little word on the media; I'm shopping arround for a new media server at the moment, we will have everything sorted out by the end of the month.
Long Story
 

Posted By: jamjar
Date: Saturday 21st August, 2004 at 12:48 GMT
  
Hey, first of all I would like to appologise for the odd 9 days of downtime, the reasons for which I'm going to explain below. At the beginning of the month you may have noticed the server being extremely slow; this was because something was wrong with apache (httpd program), dispite numerous requests however, our webhost never got round to fixing it. Atleast the website was online. Around about the 12th of this month a problem arose with some of the IP addresses on the old server, this time the web-host was not willing to admit that there was something wrong with the server, and made no attempt to fix it. This was to blame for about four days of downtime.

Following that we decided it was time to move web hosts, as all this downtime was becoming too cripling to the site's popularity. We chose X2 Servers (Big Mistake). We bought a supposed '100mbit' server for an initial price of $300. However when we found that transfer speeds we slow, we conducted a few more tests and came to find that the server was only on a 10mbit line (pretty useless for dbg.com). When we requested a refund, we were told that they could only be refunded half the ammount ($150), and that we would have to wait 8 days before we could even receive the refund. They claimed that they had paid $150 for the server to be setup.

We found out that they were re-selling from fdc servers. The server they were buying from fdc servers cost $0 to setup and cost only $150/month. At this point we knew they were screwing us big time and responded by asking them:

how come you pay more for the servers than normal customers, when you are a reseller?

All their support said was:
I honestly don't know, pricing is the decision of management. My only guess is probably they wanted more profit

They didn't even attempt to hide the fact that they were screwing us over.....Unfortunately there is not alot we can do because everything was orgonised over the internet, the most we can do is raise the issue with paypal.

So there we were on thursday; down $150 and and no server. Our options were limited; we decided to go with a well known company and pay that little bit more for better service. However with only $150 to spend our options were limited - we were only able to pay for a server with 1TB of bandwidth.

So sorry guys, that means no episodes or movies for about two weeks. 1TB will just about cover the smaller media. However this does give us an opportunity to install a better download system, which will allow you to make comments and rate media. We will also be getting a forum shortly.

So let me say this: NEVER EVER PURCHASE FROM X2 SERVERS....I personally recommends HostXL.....Whoever you by from; be sure to check them out first.

I know at this point it seems kinda rude to ask; but donations would really be appreciated.
